title: Edit Group & Subitem Terminology
excerpt: I love the feature on boards that allows owners to “Edit Item Terminology.” Please add the ability to change the terminology for groups and for subitems. Just as you are able to change the terminology for items on the bo…

posts.author: CReilly (Christina Reilly)
posts.cookedHtml:
I love the feature on boards that allows owners to “Edit Item Terminology.” Please add the ability to change the terminology for groups and for subitems. Just as you are able to change the terminology for items on the board, you should be able to do so for groups and for subitems.

For example, I have a board measuring objectives and key results. The “Group” is the “Objective,” each “Item” is a “Key Result,” and each “Subitem” is a “Step.”

image1442×995 145 KB

posts.author: crmma (Svetlana Khomich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Board item terminology is not implemented consistantly:
after renaming Items to Contacts, contextual menu should change, eg Select Contact
There should be a possibility to also change terminology for subitems. ie. Contacts —> Assistant

posts.author: MThornton (Micah Thornton)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add the ability to change the item terminology for subitems. Just as you are able to change the terminology for items on the board, you should be able to do so for subitems. For example, it would be helpful if I could change the terminology to say “Vendors” here. But “Subitem” is the only option. Thank you.

posts.author: SHK (Sam Hancock)
posts.cookedHtml: I totally agree. I use sub items to list prospective buyers (enquirers) of vintage cars that we sell. Makes no sense to not be able to change the terminology to “Enquirer” - especiallly as sub items are labelled (by default) as “Tasks” in Kanban cards.

posts.author: Usernamegame (Jake N)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT @Julietteb Could this feature request please be merged with this very similar request to accurately tally the interest/votes?

Subitem terminology Feature requests
It would be nice to be able to set the subitem terminology like you can set the item terminology on the parent board. Right now all subitems are under “Subitems”. In this example it would be nice to call them “Addresses” [subitems]

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely! Thank you for flagging

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be nice to be able to set the subitem terminology like you can set the item terminology on the parent board.

Right now all subitems are under “Subitems”. In this example it would be nice to call them “Addresses”

subitems780×240 9.93 KB

posts.author: TEAMVAERK (Morten)
posts.cookedHtml: Ohh yes! Subitem is very “techy”, so it would give a lot of flexibility if it was possible to rename.

posts.author: snicho (Stephen Nicholson)
posts.cookedHtml:
This makes a lot of sense. I guess it was just an oversight when Subitems were implemented.

However, being able to customise the Item terminology goes a long way to facilitating user buy-in;
It logically follows that we should be able to also set the terminology for Subitems.

I’d like to see the option to set it the same as the Item terminology (ie. using a checkbox) as a default, but then be able to set it to any value as per the current Item options.

Let’s hope that it doesn’t take a whole year to have this available.

posts.author: pm-chase (Chase Ferguson)
posts.cookedHtml: Whether or not you can copy terminology from items I can live without but regardless I’d love to be able to rename subitems to something else. There are so many use cases of item names and subitems names to keep your hierarchal flow but you loose that without the ability to name the subitems.

posts.author: Terry.Heard (Terry Heard)
posts.cookedHtml: Would definitely love to be able to rename the Sub Item Column Title to make it more meaningful

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Well, there goes hope… haha.

posts.author: Louie (Louie Banluta)
posts.cookedHtml: Waiting for the update featuring SubItem terminology.

posts.author: jonnyhomefixerss (Jonny K)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes!! Please allow this feature

posts.author: Paul_K (Paul Krisman)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote for this, consistency would be nice

posts.author: ksnavely (Kyle Snavely)
posts.cookedHtml: Add me to the list!

posts.author: kacper (Kacper Marciniak)
posts.cookedHtml: We’ll get GTA 6 before we get sub item terminology in Monday.com

posts.author: WellnessLoud (Wellness Loud)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d also like to see this feature updated! I know they released the Subitem automation app, but would rather like to see this as a core function instead.

posts.author: JustinSeed (Justin Seed)
posts.cookedHtml: Please implement this feature!

posts.author: gygagnon (Guy)
posts.cookedHtml: One more vote for this. Would help for user buy-in and a more bespoke experience. I can imagine this is very hard to implement.

posts.author: STNSteph (Steph Diaz)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for need to rename subitem terminology

posts.author: MichaelBagir (Michael Milis)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 to rename subitem terminology. Seems like a simple fix to me. Would be very useful

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 from another customer.

posts.author: nh44s (Noah Haas)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 yes please! This would be tremendously helpful!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Shannon here with the monday team. Thank you all for your thoughtful feedback on editing subitem terminology. We understand how helpful this customization could be to optimize your board structure.

Right now, we are focusing on making subitems more powerful and consistent with the core item experience. That work is well underway, and once it’s in place, we’re excited to explore additional enhancements like this. We truly appreciate your input, and we’ll keep you posted as things move forward!

posts.author: agualdo (Dave)
posts.cookedHtml: Still waiting … Monday.com team trying to close this topic by providing a generic statement … This request has been asked/opened since 2023 … I am sure @shannon-cxmonday … we deserve a better update on this subject … Thank you

posts.author: miekmb (Mieke Mynsberghe)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s not even on the roadmap

posts.author: oldenboom (Oldenboom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Newbee Monday user here, stumbling on this issue within hours. Then stumbing upon this thread which shows a resolution written by Monday while, on the top of the page, it says the resolution is not even on the roadmap. This doesn’t sound like a very complicated issue to resolve?

Not sure if @shannon-cxmonday is still active here as her last contribution was 4 months ago. So, I’m tagging @monday.com now as this thread might have some wrong meta-data causing it not to popup anymore at the team.

posts.author: Tom-CXmonday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Tom from monday here, stepping in for Shannon as one of the monday’s internal representation here in our community.
I want to start by saying that we hear every vote and reply loud and clear! Now, in full transparency, this feature currently remains off the immediate roadmap as our senior product team prioritizes core platform updates. That said, we always keep highly requested features in mind as we plan for the future. I appreciate your patience and the directness you’ve shown in keeping this topic alive. If we get any good news regarding this request, we’ll be sure to keep you updated!
